title: securecontext – Development Guide
text: R package memory, knowledge persistence, RAG retrieval, context window management R LLM agents.

title: securecontext – Development Guide
text: S3 classes value objects: document, retriever, context_builder, embedder R6 classes stateful stores: vector_store, knowledge_store TF-IDF embeddings work locally external API Token counting uses word-based approximation (1.3 tokens/word)

title: securecontext – Development Guide
text: test-document.R – S3 document class test-chunk.R – chunking strategies test-embeddings.R – TF-IDF embedder test-vector-store.R – vector store CRUD + search test-knowledge-store.R – JSONL persistence test-retriever.R – end--end retrieval test-context-builder.R – token-aware assembly test-token-count.R – token counting test-integration.R – orchestr/ellmer helpers

title: RAG-Enabled Agents
text: Start creating documents, chunking , loading retriever. Everything runs locally – external API calls required.
code: library(securecontext) library(orchestr) library(ellmer)  # Create documents from your corpus docs <- list(   document("R provides extensive facilities for statistical computing and graphics. Linear models, time-series analysis, classification, and clustering are all available out of the box.",     metadata = list(source = "r-intro", topic = "statistics")),   document("The tidyverse is a collection of R packages for data science. Core packages include ggplot2, dplyr, tidyr, readr, and purrr.",     metadata = list(source = "r-intro", topic = "tidyverse")),   document("Shiny is an R package for building interactive web applications. It combines R's analytical power with modern web UI components.",     metadata = list(source = "r-intro", topic = "shiny")) )  # Build embedder from the corpus corpus <- vapply(docs, function(d) d@text, character(1)) embedder <- embed_tfidf(corpus)  # Create vector store and retriever vs <- vector_store$new(dims = embedder@dims) ret <- retriever(vs, embedder) add_documents(ret, docs, chunk_strategy = "sentence")

title: RAG-Enabled Agents
text: securecontext’s knowledge_store provides persistent key-value storage backed JSONL. as_orchestr_memory() function wraps orchestr agents can use memory backend. underlying JSONL file persists across R sessions, agent memory survives restarts.
code: # Create a persistent knowledge store ks <- knowledge_store$new(path = "agent-memory.jsonl")  # Wrap it for orchestr mem <- as_orchestr_memory(ks)  # The adapter exposes get/set -- the same interface orchestr expects mem$set("user.name", "Alice") mem$set("session.topic", "data analysis") mem$get("user.name") #> [1] "Alice"

title: RAG-Enabled Agents
text: core RAG pattern : retrieve relevant context LLM call. orchestr’s graph_builder(), wire two-node graph: retrieval node followed agent node. Every user query first passes retrieval node, searches vector store assembles context string. agent node answers using context.
code:
# Node 1: retrieve relevant chunks and build context retrieve_node <- function(state, config) {   query <- state$messages[[length(state$messages)]]    # Retrieve and assemble token-limited context   result <- context_for_chat(ret, query, max_tokens = 2000, k = 5)    list(context = result$context) }  # Node 2: LLM agent that uses the retrieved context agent_node <- function(state, config) {   context <- state$context %||% ""   query <- state$messages[[length(state$messages)]]    prompt <- paste0(     "Use the following context to answer the question.

",     "Context:
", context, "

",     "Question: ", query   )    chat <- chat_anthropic(system_prompt = "You are a helpful R assistant.")   response <- chat$chat(prompt)    list(messages = list(response)) }  # Wire the graph: retrieve -> agent -> END schema <- state_schema(   messages = "append:list",   context = "character" )  graph <- graph_builder(state_schema = schema) graph$add_node("retrieve", retrieve_node) graph$add_node("agent", agent_node) graph$add_edge("retrieve", "agent") graph$add_edge("agent", END) graph$set_entry_point("retrieve")  rag_graph <- graph$compile()  # Run it result <- rag_graph$invoke(list(   messages = list("What packages are in the tidyverse?") ))

title: RAG-Enabled Agents
text: knowledge base large, retrieved chunks may exceed LLM’s context window. context_builder() controls token budget priorities. builder packs items priority order budget exhausted. Dropped items reported $excluded, can log cut. Use cb_reset() turns reuse builder:
code:
cb <- context_builder(max_tokens = 500)  # System prompt gets highest priority -- always included cb <- cb_add(cb, "You are an R expert.", priority = 10, label = "system")  # Retrieved chunks get decreasing priority by relevance score hits <- retrieve(ret, "statistical models", k = 5) for (i in seq_len(nrow(hits))) {   chunk_text <- hits$id[i]  # or look up the original text   cb <- cb_add(cb, chunk_text, priority = hits$score[i], label = hits$id[i]) }  result <- cb_build(cb) cat("Included:", paste(result$included, collapse = ", "), "
") cat("Excluded:", paste(result$excluded, collapse = ", "), "
") cat("Total tokens:", result$total_tokens, "
") cb <- cb_reset(cb) # Now add fresh content for the next turn

title: RAG-Enabled Agents
text: agents need memory across sessions, knowledge_store persists JSONL file. Combined orchestr memory adapter, gives agents durable recall.
code: # Knowledge store persists to disk ks <- knowledge_store$new(path = "long-term-memory.jsonl")  # Store structured facts ks$set("user.preference", list(language = "R", theme = "dark")) ks$set("session.2025-01-15", list(   topic = "regression models",   outcome = "built linear model for mtcars" ))  # Search by key pattern ks$search("^session") #> $`session.2025-01-15` #> $`session.2025-01-15`$topic #> [1] "regression models"  # Use in an orchestr agent via the adapter mem <- as_orchestr_memory(ks)

title: RAG-Enabled Agents
text: complete RAG agent combines retrieval, token management, persistent memory orchestr graph. retrieval node finds chunks tidyr’s pivot_longer, context builder fits within token budget, agent answers using grounded context. query also persisted knowledge store future reference.
code:
library(securecontext) library(orchestr) library(ellmer)  # --- Knowledge base --- docs <- list(   document("dplyr provides a grammar of data manipulation with verbs like filter, select, mutate, summarise, and arrange."),   document("ggplot2 implements the grammar of graphics. Build plots layer by layer with aes(), geom_point(), geom_line(), and facet_wrap()."),   document("tidyr helps tidy data with pivot_longer, pivot_wider, separate, and unite.") )  corpus <- vapply(docs, function(d) d@text, character(1)) embedder <- embed_tfidf(corpus) vs <- vector_store$new(dims = embedder@dims) ret <- retriever(vs, embedder) add_documents(ret, docs, chunk_strategy = "sentence")  # --- Persistent memory --- ks <- knowledge_store$new(path = tempfile(fileext = ".jsonl")) mem <- as_orchestr_memory(ks)  # --- Graph nodes --- retrieve_node <- function(state, config) {   query <- state$messages[[length(state$messages)]]   result <- context_for_chat(ret, query, max_tokens = 1500, k = 3)   list(context = result$context) }  agent_node <- function(state, config) {   context <- state$context %||% ""   query <- state$messages[[length(state$messages)]]    prompt <- paste0("Context:
", context, "

Question: ", query)   chat <- chat_anthropic(     system_prompt = "Answer questions about R packages using the provided context."   )   response <- chat$chat(prompt)    # Persist what we learned   mem$set(paste0("query.", Sys.time()), query)    list(messages = list(response)) }  # --- Build and run --- schema <- state_schema(messages = "append:list", context = "character") g <- graph_builder(state_schema = schema) g$add_node("retrieve", retrieve_node) g$add_node("agent", agent_node) g$add_edge("retrieve", "agent") g$add_edge("agent", END) g$set_entry_point("retrieve")  rag <- g$compile() result <- rag$invoke(list(   messages = list("How do I reshape data from wide to long format?") ))

title: Retrieval Workflows
text: document() wraps raw text metadata auto-generated identifier. Documents S7 objects, properties accessed @.
code: doc_r <- document(   "R is a programming language for statistical computing and graphics. It is widely used among statisticians and data scientists. R provides a wide variety of statistical and graphical techniques, including linear and nonlinear modelling, classical statistical tests, time-series analysis, classification, and clustering.",   metadata = list(source = "intro", topic = "R") )  doc_python <- document(   "Python is a high-level, general-purpose programming language. Its design philosophy emphasizes code readability. Python supports multiple programming paradigms, including structured, object-oriented, and functional programming. It is often used for machine learning, web development, and data analysis.",   metadata = list(source = "intro", topic = "Python") )  doc_julia <- document(   "Julia is a high-level, high-performance programming language for technical computing. It was designed for numerical analysis and computational science. Julia features optional typing, multiple dispatch, and good performance approaching that of statically-typed languages like C and Fortran.",   metadata = list(source = "intro", topic = "Julia") )  # S7 property access with @ doc_r@id #> [1] "doc_1e7a339c22ad" doc_r@metadata #> $source #> [1] "intro" #>  #> $topic #> [1] "R"

title: Retrieval Workflows
text: Chunking splits long text smaller pieces suitable embedding retrieval. securecontext offers four strategies: Fixed-size chunking useful need consistent chunk lengths, example working models expect uniform input sizes:
code:
# Sentence-level chunking sentences <- chunk_text(doc_r@text, strategy = "sentence") cat("Sentence chunks:", length(sentences), "
") #> Sentence chunks: 3 sentences #> [1] "R is a programming language for statistical computing and graphics."                                                                                                                                #> [2] "It is widely used among statisticians and data scientists."                                                                                                                                         #> [3] "R provides
a wide variety of statistical and graphical techniques, including
linear and nonlinear modelling, classical statistical tests, time-series
analysis, classification, and clustering."  # Recursive chunking with a small max_size to demonstrate splitting small_chunks <- chunk_text(doc_r@text, strategy = "recursive", max_size = 120) cat("
Recursive chunks (max 120 chars):", length(small_chunks), "
") #>  #> Recursive chunks (max 120 chars): 5 small_chunks #> [1] "R is a programming language for statistical computing and graphics."      #> [2] "It is widely used among statisticians and data scientists. R provides"    #> [3] "a wide variety of statistical and graphical techniques, including"        #> [4] "linear and nonlinear modelling, classical statistical tests, time-series" #> [5] "analysis, classification, and clustering." long_text <- paste(   "The tidyverse is a collection of R packages designed for data science.",   "It includes ggplot2 for visualization, dplyr for data manipulation,",   "tidyr for data tidying, readr for reading data, purrr for functional",   "programming, tibble for modern data frames, stringr for string",   "manipulation, and forcats for factor handling.",   "All tidyverse packages share an underlying design philosophy,",   "grammar, and data structures." )  fixed_chunks <- chunk_fixed(long_text, size = 100, overlap = 20) cat("Fixed chunks (size=100, overlap=20):", length(fixed_chunks), "

") #> Fixed chunks (size=100, overlap=20): 5 for (i in seq_along(fixed_chunks)) {   cat(sprintf("Chunk %d (%d chars): %s

", i, nchar(fixed_chunks[i]), fixed_chunks[i])) } #> Chunk 1 (100 chars): The tidyverse is a collection of R packages designed for data science. It includes ggplot2 for visua #>  #> Chunk 2 (100 chars): es ggplot2 for visualization, dplyr for data manipulation, tidyr for data tidying, readr for reading #>  #> Chunk 3 (100 chars): g, readr for reading data, purrr for functional programming, tibble for modern data frames, stringr  #>  #> Chunk 4 (100 chars): ata frames, stringr for string manipulation, and forcats for factor handling. All tidyverse packages #>  #> Chunk 5 (89 chars): l tidyverse packages share an underlying design philosophy, grammar, and data structures.

title: Retrieval Workflows
text: embed_tfidf() builds vocabulary corpus returns embedder can project new texts TF-IDF space. Everything runs locally – API keys required.
code: # Gather all document texts as the training corpus corpus <- c(doc_r@text, doc_python@text, doc_julia@text)  embedder <- embed_tfidf(corpus)  # The embedder is an S7 object; dims equals the vocabulary size cat("Embedding dimensions:", embedder@dims, "\n") #> Embedding dimensions: 79  # Embed a new query query_matrix <- embed_texts(embedder, "statistical analysis in R") cat("Query embedding shape:", nrow(query_matrix), "x", ncol(query_matrix), "\n") #> Query embedding shape: 1 x 79

title: Retrieval Workflows
text: vector_store R6 class providing -memory cosine-similarity search optional RDS persistence. Since R6, use $ method access. Persistence straightforward $save() $load():
code: vs <- vector_store$new(dims = embedder@dims)  # Embed and store each document manually ids <- c("r", "python", "julia") embeddings <- embed_texts(embedder, corpus) vs$add(ids, embeddings)  cat("Store size:", vs$size(), "vectors\n") #> Store size: 3 vectors  # Search for the closest match to a query query_emb <- embed_texts(embedder, "data science and statistics") results <- vs$search(query_emb, k = 3) print(results) #>       id      score #> 1 python 0.07428944 #> 2      r 0.06359400 #> 3  julia 0.00000000 tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".rds") vs$save(tmp)  vs2 <- vector_store$new(dims = embedder@dims) vs2$load(tmp) cat("Loaded store size:", vs2$size(), "\n") #> Loaded store size: 3  # Clean up unlink(tmp)

title: Retrieval Workflows
text: retriever() combines vector store embedder single object. Use add_documents() chunk, embed, store documents one call, retrieve() search. returned data frame contains chunk IDs cosine similarity scores. Higher scores indicate greater relevance.
code: # Fresh store for the retriever vs_ret <- vector_store$new(dims = embedder@dims) ret <- retriever(vs_ret, embedder)  # add_documents handles chunking + embedding internally docs <- list(doc_r, doc_python, doc_julia) add_documents(ret, docs, chunk_strategy = "sentence")  cat("Chunks in store:", vs_ret$size(), "\n\n") #> Chunks in store: 10  # Retrieve the top 3 chunks for a query hits <- retrieve(ret, "machine learning", k = 3) print(hits) #>                         id     score #> 1 doc_1e7a26b68e48_chunk_4 0.3992843 #> 2 doc_1e7a339c22ad_chunk_1 0.0000000 #> 3 doc_1e7a339c22ad_chunk_2 0.0000000

title: Retrieval Workflows
text: knowledge_store R6 class providing persistent key-value storage backed JSONL. useful storing agent memory, user preferences, structured data survive across sessions.
code: ks <- knowledge_store$new(path = tempfile(fileext = ".jsonl"))  # Store some facts ks$set("lang.r", list(type = "statistical", year = 1993)) ks$set("lang.python", list(type = "general-purpose", year = 1991)) ks$set("lang.julia", list(type = "numerical", year = 2012)) ks$set("user.preference", "R")  cat("Total entries:", ks$size(), "\n") #> Total entries: 4  # Retrieve a value ks$get("lang.r") #> $type #> [1] "statistical" #>  #> $year #> [1] 1993  # Search keys by regex ks$search("^lang") #> [1] "lang.r"      "lang.python" "lang.julia"  # List all keys ks$list() #> [1] "lang.r"          "lang.python"     "lang.julia"      "user.preference"  # Clean up unlink(ks$.__enclos_env__$private$.path)

title: Retrieval Workflows
text: context_builder() assembles token-limited context string multiple sources, prioritizing important content. final step sending context LLM. builder processes items priority order stops adding token budget exhausted. Items fit reported $excluded, making easy log dropped. Use cb_reset() clear items reuse builder new turn conversation:
code:
cb <- context_builder(max_tokens = 100)  # Add content with different priorities (higher = included first) cb <- cb_add(cb, "You are a helpful assistant.", priority = 10, label = "system") cb <- cb_add(cb,   "R is great for statistics and data visualization.",   priority = 5, label = "retrieved_chunk_1" ) cb <- cb_add(cb,   "Python is popular for machine learning and web development.",   priority = 4, label = "retrieved_chunk_2" ) cb <- cb_add(cb,   "Julia offers high performance for numerical computing workloads.",   priority = 3, label = "retrieved_chunk_3" )  result <- cb_build(cb)  cat("Assembled context:
") #> Assembled context: cat(result$context, "

") #> You are a helpful assistant. #>  #> R is great for statistics and data visualization. #>  #> Python is popular for machine learning and web development. #>  #> Julia offers high performance for numerical computing workloads. cat("Included:", paste(result$included, collapse = ", "), "
") #> Included: system, retrieved_chunk_1, retrieved_chunk_2, retrieved_chunk_3 cat("Excluded:", paste(result$excluded, collapse = ", "), "
") #> Excluded: cat("Total tokens:", result$total_tokens, "
") #> Total tokens: 41 cb2 <- cb_reset(cb) cb2 <- cb_add(cb2, "New system prompt.", priority = 10, label = "system_v2") result2 <- cb_build(cb2) cat("After reset -- included:", paste(result2$included, collapse = ", "), "
") #> After reset -- included: system_v2

what: Full Pipeline: Retrieve and Build Context
title: Retrieval Workflows
text: context_for_chat() combines retrieval context building single call. Given retriever query, retrieves top-k chunks packs token-limited context string. typical integration point agent: retrieve relevant information, assemble context window, pass LLM provider (via ellmer, httr2, HTTP client).
code:
context_result <- context_for_chat(ret, "statistical computing", max_tokens = 2000, k = 5)  cat("Context for LLM:
") #> Context for LLM: cat(context_result$context, "

") #> R is a programming language for statistical computing and graphics. #>  #> R provides #> a wide variety of statistical and graphical techniques, including #> linear and nonlinear modelling, classical statistical tests, time-series #> analysis, classification, and clustering. #>  #> It is widely used among statisticians and data scientists. #>  #> Python is a high-level, general-purpose programming language. #>  #> Its design philosophy emphasizes code readability. cat("Included chunks:", length(context_result$included), "
") #> Included chunks: 5 cat("Total tokens:", context_result$total_tokens, "
") #> Total tokens: 73

title: Retrieval Workflows
text: securecontext retrieval pipeline follows steps: document() – wrap text metadata chunk_text() – split retrieval units embed_tfidf() – build local embedder corpus vector_store$new() – store search embeddings retriever() + add_documents() – high-level ingest retrieve() – semantic search knowledge_store$new() – persistent key-value memory context_builder() + cb_add() + cb_build() – token-aware assembly context_for_chat() – one-call retrieve--build

title: Getting Started with securecontext
code: library(securecontext)  doc <- document(   "R is a language for statistical computing. It has many packages for data analysis. The tidyverse is a popular collection of packages.",   metadata = list(source = "intro") )  # Different chunking strategies chunks_sent <- chunk_text(doc$text, strategy = "sentence") chunks_para <- chunk_text(doc$text, strategy = "paragraph") chunks_rec  <- chunk_text(doc$text, strategy = "recursive", max_size = 50)

title: Getting Started with securecontext
code: corpus <- c(   "R is great for statistics and data analysis.",   "Python excels at machine learning and deep learning.",   "Julia is fast for numerical computing." )  embedder <- embed_tfidf(corpus) vs <- vector_store$new(dims = embedder$dims)  # Add documents docs <- lapply(corpus, document) ret <- retriever(vs, embedder) add_documents(ret, docs)  # Search results <- retrieve(ret, "statistical computing", k = 2) print(results)

title: Getting Started with securecontext
code: ks <- knowledge_store$new(path = tempfile(fileext = ".jsonl"))  ks$set("user.name", "Alice") ks$set("user.preferences", list(theme = "dark", lang = "R"))  ks$get("user.name") ks$search("^user")

title: Getting Started with securecontext
code: cb <- context_builder(max_tokens = 500) cb <- cb_add(cb, "System instructions go here.", priority = 10, label = "system") cb <- cb_add(cb, "Retrieved context from documents.", priority = 5, label = "context") cb <- cb_add(cb, "Chat history summary.", priority = 3, label = "history")  result <- cb_build(cb) cat(result$context) result$included result$excluded

title: Getting Started with securecontext
text: Use context_for_chat() combine retrieval context building: Wrap knowledge store orchestr:
code: result <- context_for_chat(ret, "statistics", max_tokens = 2000) cat(result$context) mem <- as_orchestr_memory(ks) mem$set("key", "value") mem$get("key")

title: Memory, Knowledge Persistence, and Context Management for R LLM Agents
text: securecontext part 7-package ecosystem building governed AI agents R: securecontext provides memory retrieval layer agents. sits alongside securetools secureguard middle tier, giving agents ability chunk documents, build TF-IDF embeddings locally, retrieve relevant context LLM prompts.

title: Memory, Knowledge Persistence, and Context Management for R LLM Agents
Memory, Knowledge Persistence, and Context Management for R LLM Agents
text: Document chunking – fixed-size, sentence, paragraph, recursive strategies TF-IDF embeddings – local embeddings external API required Vector store – -memory cosine similarity search RDS persistence Knowledge store – persistent JSONL key-value storage Semantic retrieval – query documents meaning Context builder – token-aware priority-based context assembly Integration helpers – works orchestr ellmer

title: Memory, Knowledge Persistence, and Context Management for R LLM Agents
code: library(securecontext)  # Create documents docs <- list(   document("R is great for statistics.", metadata = list(topic = "R")),   document("Python excels at machine learning.", metadata = list(topic = "Python")) )  # Build embeddings and index documents emb <- embed_tfidf(vapply(docs, `[[`, character(1), "text")) vs <- vector_store$new(dims = emb$dims) ret <- retriever(vs, emb) add_documents(ret, docs)  # Retrieve relevant context result <- context_for_chat(ret, "statistical computing", max_tokens = 2000) cat(result$context)

Add content to a context builder — cb_add
Add content to a context builder — cb_add
text: builder securecontext_context_builder. text Character string add. priority Numeric priority (higher = included first). label Optional label tracking included/excluded.

title: Add content to a context builder — cb_add
Add content to a context builder — cb_add
code: cb <- context_builder(max_tokens = 100) cb <- cb_add(cb, "High priority text", priority = 10, label = "important") cb <- cb_add(cb, "Low priority text", priority = 1, label = "filler")

title: Build the context string — cb_build
text: Assembles context including highest-priority items first token limit reached.

title: Build the context string — cb_build
text: list elements context (assembled string), included (labels included items), excluded (labels excluded items), total_tokens (token count assembled context).

title: Build the context string — cb_build
code: cb <- context_builder(max_tokens = 100) cb <- cb_add(cb, "Important info", priority = 10) result <- cb_build(cb) result$context #> [1] "Important info"

title: Reset a context builder — cb_reset
code: cb <- context_builder(max_tokens = 100) cb <- cb_add(cb, "some text") cb <- cb_reset(cb) length(cb@items) #> [1] 0

title: Fixed-size text chunking — chunk_fixed
text: Splits text chunks approximately size characters optional overlap.

title: Fixed-size text chunking — chunk_fixed
text: text Character string chunk. size Target chunk size characters. overlap Number overlap characters consecutive chunks.

title: Fixed-size text chunking — chunk_fixed
code: chunk_fixed(paste(rep("word", 200), collapse = " "), size = 100, overlap = 10) #>  [1] "word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word " #>  [2] "word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word " #>  [3] "word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word " #>  [4] "word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word " #>  [5] "word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word " #>  [6] "word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word " #>  [7] "word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word " #>  [8] "word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word " #>  [9] "word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word " #> [10] "word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word " #> [11] "word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word word"

title: Recursive text chunking — chunk_recursive
text: Recursively splits text using hierarchy separators, similar LangChain's recursive text splitter.

title: Recursive text chunking — chunk_recursive
text: text Character string chunk. max_size Maximum chunk size characters. separators Character vector separators try order.

title: Recursive text chunking — chunk_recursive
code: long_text <- paste(rep("This is a sentence.", 20), collapse = " ") chunk_recursive(long_text, max_size = 80) #>  [1] "This is a sentence"  "This is a sentence"  "This is a sentence"  #>  [4] "This is a sentence"  "This is a sentence"  "This is a sentence"  #>  [7] "This is a sentence"  "This is a sentence"  "This is a sentence"  #> [10] "This is a sentence"  "This is a sentence"  "This is a sentence"  #> [13] "This is a sentence"  "This is a sentence"  "This is a sentence"  #> [16] "This is a sentence"  "This is a sentence"  "This is a sentence"  #> [19] "This is a sentence"  "This is a sentence."

title: Sentence-based text chunking — chunk_sentence
text: Splits text sentence boundaries (period followed space newline).

title: Sentence-based text chunking — chunk_sentence
code: chunk_sentence("First sentence. Second sentence. Third one.") #> [1] "First sentence."  "Second sentence." "Third one."

what: Chunk text into smaller pieces — chunk_text
title: Chunk text into smaller pieces — chunk_text
Chunk text into smaller pieces — chunk_text
Chunk text into smaller pieces — chunk_text
text: text Character string chunk. strategy Chunking strategy: "fixed", "sentence", "paragraph", "recursive". ... Additional arguments passed strategy function.

title: Chunk text into smaller pieces — chunk_text
Chunk text into smaller pieces — chunk_text
code: chunks <- chunk_text("Hello world. How are you?", strategy = "sentence")

title: Create a context builder — context_builder
text: max_tokens Maximum number tokens assembled context.

title: Create a context builder — context_builder
code: cb <- context_builder(max_tokens = 100) cb <- cb_add(cb, "Important info", priority = 10) cb <- cb_add(cb, "Less important", priority = 1) result <- cb_build(cb)

what: Build context for LLM chat — context_for_chat
title: Build context for LLM chat — context_for_chat
text: Convenience function retrieves relevant chunks builds token-limited context string.

title: Build context for LLM chat — context_for_chat
Build context for LLM chat — context_for_chat
text: ret securecontext_retriever object. query Character string query. max_tokens Maximum tokens context. k Number chunks retrieve.

title: Build context for LLM chat — context_for_chat
Build context for LLM chat — context_for_chat
code: emb <- embed_tfidf(c("cat sat on mat", "dog ran in park")) vs <- vector_store$new(dims = emb@dims) ret <- retriever(vs, emb) add_documents(ret, document("The cat sat on the mat.")) result <- context_for_chat(ret, "cat", max_tokens = 100, k = 2) result$context #> [1] "The cat sat on the mat."

title: Count tokens in text — count_tokens
text: Approximates number tokens using either word-based character-based method.

title: Count tokens in text — count_tokens
text: text Character string (vector) count tokens . method Counting method: "words" (default) multiplies word count 1.3; "chars" divides character count 4.

title: Count tokens in text — count_tokens
code: count_tokens("Hello world, this is a test.") #> [1] 8

title: Create a document — document
text: Constructs S7 object representing text document metadata.

title: Create a document — document
text: text Character string document content. metadata Named list arbitrary metadata. id Optional document identifier. Generated NULL.

title: Create a document — document
code: doc <- document("Hello world", metadata = list(source = "test")) doc #> <securecontext::securecontext_document> #>  @ text    : chr "Hello world" #>  @ metadata:List of 1 #>  .. $ source: chr "test" #>  @ id      : chr "doc_1bd22935c4c2"

what: Embed texts using an embedder — embed_texts
title: Embed texts using an embedder — embed_texts
Embed texts using an embedder — embed_texts
Embed texts using an embedder — embed_texts
text: embedder securecontext_embedder object. texts Character vector texts embed.

title: Embed texts using an embedder — embed_texts
text: Numeric matrix length(texts) rows embedder@dims columns.

title: Embed texts using an embedder — embed_texts
code: emb <- embed_tfidf(c("the cat sat", "the dog ran")) mat <- embed_texts(emb, c("cat sat", "dog ran")) nrow(mat) #> [1] 2

title: Create a TF-IDF embedder — embed_tfidf
text: Builds TF-IDF vocabulary corpus returns embedder can embed new texts vocabulary space. external API required.

title: Create a TF-IDF embedder — embed_tfidf
text: corpus Character vector documents build vocabulary .

title: Create a TF-IDF embedder — embed_tfidf
code: emb <- embed_tfidf(c("the cat sat", "the dog ran"))

title: Estimate token count — estimate_tokens
code: estimate_tokens("Hello world, this is a test.") #> [1] 8

what: Test if object is a document — is_document
title: Test if object is a document — is_document
Test if object is a document — is_document
Test if object is a document — is_document
Test if object is a document — is_document
Test if object is a document — is_document
code: doc <- document("Hello world") is_document(doc) #> [1] TRUE is_document("not a doc") #> [1] FALSE

title: Knowledge Store — knowledge_store
text: Persistent JSONL key-value knowledge base. entry stores key, value, optional metadata, timestamp.

title: Knowledge Store — knowledge_store
text: knowledge_store$new() knowledge_store$set() knowledge_store$get() knowledge_store$delete() knowledge_store$search() knowledge_store$list() knowledge_store$size() knowledge_store$save() knowledge_store$load() knowledge_store$clone()

title: Knowledge Store — knowledge_store
text: path Optional file path JSONL persistence. NULL -memory .

title: Knowledge Store — knowledge_store
text: key Character key. value R object can serialized JSON. metadata Named list metadata.

title: Knowledge Store — knowledge_store
text: key Character key. default Value return key found.

title: Knowledge Store — knowledge_store
code: ks <- knowledge_store$new() ks$set("color", "blue", metadata = list(source = "test")) ks$get("color") #> [1] "blue" ks$search("col") #> [1] "color" ks$size() #> [1] 1

title: Create an embedder — new_embedder
text: embed_fn function taking character vector returning numeric matrix one row per text dims columns. dims Integer, dimensionality embedding space.

title: Create an embedder — new_embedder
code: # Create a simple random embedder random_embed <- function(texts) matrix(runif(length(texts) * 3), ncol = 3) emb <- new_embedder(random_embed, dims = 3L) emb@dims #> [1] 3

title: Retrieve relevant chunks — retrieve
text: Embeds query, searches vector store, returns results.

title: Retrieve relevant chunks — retrieve
text: ret securecontext_retriever object. query Character string query. k Number results.

title: Retrieve relevant chunks — retrieve
code: emb <- embed_tfidf(c("cat sat on mat", "dog ran in park")) vs <- vector_store$new(dims = emb@dims) ret <- retriever(vs, emb) add_documents(ret, document("The cat sat on the mat.")) retrieve(ret, "cat", k = 1) #>                         id     score #> 1 doc_1bd210792c39_chunk_1 0.5773503

title: Create a retriever — retriever
text: store vector_store object. embedder securecontext_embedder object.

title: Create a retriever — retriever
code: emb <- embed_tfidf(c("cat sat on mat", "dog ran in park")) vs <- vector_store$new(dims = emb@dims) ret <- retriever(vs, emb)

what: securecontext: Memory, Knowledge Persistence, and Context Management for R LLM Agents — securecontext-package
title: securecontext: Memory, Knowledge Persistence, and Context Management for R LLM Agents — securecontext-package
text: Provides memory, knowledge persistence, RAG retrieval, token-aware context window management R LLM agents. Includes document chunking, TF-IDF embeddings, vector search, persistent key-value knowledge stores, priority-based context assembly.

title: securecontext: Memory, Knowledge Persistence, and Context Management for R LLM Agents — securecontext-package

code: doc <- securecontext_document(   text = "Sample text", metadata = list(source = "test"), id = "doc1" ) doc@text #> [1] "Sample text"

S7 class for securecontext retrievers — securecontext_retriever
text: store vector_store object. embedder securecontext_embedder object.

title: S7 class for securecontext retrievers — securecontext_retriever
code: emb <- embed_tfidf(c("cat sat on mat", "dog ran in park")) vs <- vector_store$new(dims = emb@dims) ret <- retriever(vs, emb) ret@embedder #> <securecontext::securecontext_embedder> #>  @ embed_fn: function (texts)   #>  @ dims    : int 8

title: Vector Store — vector_store
text: -memory vector store cosine similarity search RDS persistence.

title: Vector Store — vector_store
text: vector_store$new() vector_store$add() vector_store$search() vector_store$remove() vector_store$size() vector_store$save() vector_store$load() vector_store$clone()

title: Vector Store — vector_store
text: ids Character vector unique identifiers. embeddings Numeric matrix (nrow = length(ids), ncol = dims). metadata List metadata entries (one per id), empty list.

title: Vector Store — vector_store
text: query_embedding Numeric vector single-row matrix. k Number results return.

title: Vector Store — vector_store
text: Data frame columns id, score, metadata.

title: Vector Store — vector_store
code: vs <- vector_store$new(dims = 3L) vs$add("id1", matrix(c(1, 0, 0), nrow = 1)) vs$add("id2", matrix(c(0, 1, 0), nrow = 1)) vs$search(c(1, 0, 0), k = 1) #>    id score #> 1 id1     1 vs$size() #> [1] 2
